raw_consumer_statement:
I am submitting this complaint regarding Barclays Bank Delaware and its handling of a fraudulent transaction dispute on my Barclays AAdvantage Aviator Red Mastercard. 
On or about XX/XX/year>, my wallet was stolen. The wallet contained my Barclays credit card, along with other financial cards. I did not have possession of my Barclays card at the time the disputed transactions occurred. 
After discovering the theft, I identified the following unauthorized transactions on my account : XX/XX/year>XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$360.00} XX/XX/year>XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$110.00} XX/XX/year> XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX {$100.00} These charges total {$590.00} and were not authorized by me. I did not participate in or benefit from these transactions. 
Barclays has denied or is disputing my fraud claim based on an alleged statement that I had possession of my card at the time of the transactions. This is incorrect. At no point did I knowingly or intentionally state that I had possession of my card after my wallet was stolen. Any such assertion is either a misunderstanding, mischaracterization, or documentation error on the part of Barclays. 
To be clear : My wallet, including my Barclays card, was stolen on or about XX/XX/year>. 
I did not have possession of the card when these transactions occurred. 
I did not authorize these transactions. 
Additionally, I have filed a police report documenting the theft and the resulting fraudulent use of my credit card. 
Under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), I am not liable for unauthorized charges made on a lost or stolen credit card. Barclays reliance on an inaccurate or misinterpreted phone statement does not override the facts of this case or my rights under federal law.
